ucs.title: [LLM] APT5 (Mulberry Typhoon / UNC2630) post-VPN-appliance lateral pivot: RDP/SMB from edge gateway followed by LSASS dumping
ucs.description: Hunts the characteristic APT5 chain of compromising a Pulse Secure / Citrix ADC / Fortinet edge appliance via T1190 zero-day, then pivoting from the appliance's management/internal interface to a domain host where they immediately stage credential theft (Mandiant: SLOWPULSE, RADIALPULSE, ATRIUM; NSA APT5 Citrix hunting bulletin Dec 2022). The behavior of a logon/SMB session originating from the VPN concentrator's inside IP followed within minutes by lsass handle access on the same host is highly specific to APT5's known op tempo on telco/defense targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen values(Authentication.src) as src values(Authentication.user) as user from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action=success Authentication.app=win:remote* [| inputlookup apt5_edge_appliance_ips.csv | fields src_ip | rename src_ip as Authentication.src] by Authentication.dest Authentication.src _time span=1h | rename Authentication.* as * | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("rundll32.exe","procdump.exe","comsvcs.dll","taskmgr.exe","powershell.exe","sqldumper.exe") (Processes.process=*lsass* OR Processes.process=*MiniDump* OR Processes.process=*sekurlsa* OR Processes.process=*comsvcs.dll*MiniDump*) by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name _time span=1h | rename Processes.* as * | rename _time as cred_dump_time] | where cred_dump_time >= first_seen AND cred_dump_time <= (last_seen + 3600) | table first_seen cred_dump_time src dest user process_name process parent_process_name
ucs.kql:
let edgeAppliances = dynamic(["10.10.250.5","10.10.250.6"]); // <- populate with Pulse/Citrix/Fortinet inside IPs
let pivotWindow = 1h;
let pivots =
  IdentityLogonEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
  | where ActionType == "LogonSuccess"
  | where LogonType in ("RemoteInteractive","Network")
  | where IPAddress in (edgeAppliances)
  | project PivotTime=Timestamp, DeviceName=DestinationDeviceName, AccountName, IPAddress, LogonType;
let credDumps =
  DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
  | where (FileName in~ ("rundll32.exe","procdump.exe","taskmgr.exe","powershell.exe","sqldumper.exe","createdump.exe"))
      and (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("lsass","MiniDump","sekurlsa","comsvcs.dll, MiniDump","-ma ","SilentProcessExit"))
  | project DumpTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, DumpProc=FileName, DumpCmd=ProcessCommandLine, DumpUser=AccountName, ParentName=InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine
  | union (
     DeviceEvents
     | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
     | where ActionType == "OpenProcessApiCall"
     | extend tp = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).TargetImageFileName)
     | where tp endswith "lsass.exe"
     | project DumpTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, DumpProc=InitiatingProcessFileName, DumpCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine, DumpUser=InitiatingProcessAccountName, ParentName=InitiatingProcessParentFileName, ParentCmd=""
  );
pivots
| join kind=inner credDumps on DeviceName
| where DumpTime between (PivotTime .. PivotTime + pivotWindow)
| project PivotTime, DumpTime, DeviceName, AccountName, IPAddress, DumpProc, DumpCmd, DumpUser, ParentName
| order by PivotTime desc
ucs.rationale: APT5's signature initial-access pattern across a decade is exploitation of internet-facing networking devices (Pulse Secure CVE-2019-11510 / CVE-2021-22893, Citrix ADC CVE-2022-27518, Fortinet FortiOS) — confirmed by Mandiant, NSA, and Microsoft (Mulberry Typhoon) reporting. Because most orgs do not run EDR on the appliance itself, the highest-fidelity detection is the moment APT5 leaves the appli

ucs.title: [LLM] APT5 web shell child-process hunt on Citrix ADC / Pulse / IIS gateway hosts
ucs.description: Detects APT5's post-exploit drop of web shells and trojanized appliance binaries (SLIGHTPULSE, RADIALPULSE, ATRIUM web shells; per Mandiant UNC2630 and NSA Citrix hunt guide) by alerting on internet-facing gateway processes (w3wp.exe, httpd, nsppe, dsmdm) spawning Windows/Unix shells, recon binaries, or archive utilities. Targets the actor's documented combination of T1190 → T1505.003 → T1083/T1049/T1057 reconnaissance.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.process_id) as pid values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat*.exe","java.exe","nsppe","nsapimgr","dsmdm","web") Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","sh","bash","perl","python","python3","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","ifconfig","netstat.exe","netstat","arp.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","reg.exe","rar.exe","7z.exe","wmic.exe","schtasks.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | rename Processes.* as * | eval risk_score = case(process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","reg.exe","rar.exe","7z.exe","wmic.exe"), 70, process_name IN ("net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","schtasks.exe"), 60, 1=1, 40) | where risk_score >= 40 | sort -lastTime | table firstTime lastTime dest user parent_process_name process_name cmd parent_cmd risk_score count
ucs.kql:
let gatewayParents = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat.exe","tomcat9.exe","java.exe","node.exe","php-cgi.exe"]);
let reconChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","netstat.exe","arp.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","reg.exe","rar.exe","7z.exe","wmic.exe","schtasks.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
let susp =
  DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (gatewayParents)
  | where FileName in~ (reconChildren)
  | extend Score = case(
      FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rar.exe","7z.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"), 80,
      FileName in~ ("net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","reg.exe","wmic.exe","schtasks.exe"), 60,
      40)
  | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, Score, ReportId;
let edgeFacing =
  DeviceNetworkEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
  | where ActionType == "InboundConnectionAccepted"
  | where LocalPort in (80,443,8443,4443)
  | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
  | summarize PublicHits=count() by DeviceName
  | where PublicHits > 50;
susp
| join kind=inner edgeFacing on DeviceName
| extend AlertScore = Score + 20 // bonus: this host is genuinely internet-exposed
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FolderPath has_any ("\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\","\\netscaler\\","/var/vpn/","/home/perl/","/var/dana/")
    | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".jsp" or FileName endswith ".php" or FileName endswith ".pl" or FileName endswith ".cgi"
    | project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, DroppedShell=FolderPath + FileName, FileSize
  ) on DeviceName
| where isnotempty(DroppedShell) or AlertScore >= 80
| order by AlertScore desc, Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: APT5/UNC2630 has an unusually consistent post-exploitation pattern on edge gateways: after exploiting a public-facing appliance (Pulse, Citrix ADC, Fortinet, Exchange) they deploy ASPX/JSP/Perl web shells (ATRIUM, SLIGHTPULSE) and immediately enumerate the host with net.exe / nltest / netstat / quser before pivoting. Most orgs cannot get EDR onto the appliance OS, but the same TTP appears on Windo
